segments.text: What is the fastest winter Olympic sport? Clocking in at up to 55 km an hour are Olympic speed skaters. That's about as fast as a tiger can run. All
while balancing on a skate a millimeter thick. Next up are ski jumpers that soar down a 35° ramp and reach takeoff speeds that are double that of a speed skater.
But they're not as fast as snowboarders.
In snowboard cross, they whiz down tracks with jumps and mogul and curves.
And they can be going as fast as a car on a highway. but they can't touch alpine skiers who can regularly get up to 120 km [music] per hour. But then
there's a sport I didn't know existed called skeleton where athletes go headirst at 135 km an hour and
experience gforces similar to what astronauts feel when launching into space and even faster. Bob [music]
sledders fly down in a rocket shaped sled where teams of four can hit the insane speed of 145 km an hour. But the
fastest sport of all. Have you guessed it? While you think about it, [music]
subscribe. The answer is people lying on their backs, feet first, face up, going 150 km [music] an hour. Bluj is the
